findings.summary: typescript ^5.9.5 guessed from memory; version does not exist
findings.fix: registry-before-versions rule in CLAUDE.md

findings.summary: local bun 1.3.9 vs CI 'latest' 1.3.14 broke CI on first push
findings.fix: doctor.mjs pin check + CI pin + engines field

findings.summary: bun test empty-suite exit code differs macOS vs Linux
findings.fix: conditional test gate, self-removing at P0-2

findings.summary: five gates existed with no single runner; manual emulation error-prone
findings.fix: scripts/gates.mjs, CI runs identical script

findings.summary: stray nested .gitignore from persisted cd; pattern matched nothing
findings.fix: deleted; root .gitignore carries the pattern

findings.summary: PackManifest capability generator exercised 7 of 11 enum values
findings.fix: generator extended to full enum

findings.summary: kernel_compat validated as bare string, not semver range
findings.fix: SemverRange scalar in @kelson/schemas; PackManifest.kernel_compat uses it; refusal fixtures in PACK-1.test.ts

findings.summary: ERD §2 declared drift_event append-only while §3 requires resolution to mutate
findings.fix: ERD §2 amended: drift_event is insert-then-resolve

findings.summary: ERD §3 DRIFT_EVENT PK comment still said append-only after §2 amendment (ripple miss)
findings.fix: comment updated in same commit

findings.summary: artifact composite PK (repo, logical_id) in DDL diverged from ERD's logical_id-only PK
findings.fix: ERD §3 amended to composite key (code was right, spec followed)

findings.summary: schema_version missing on intervention_event/drift_event; spec-internal contradiction resolved silently toward ERD §5
findings.fix: column added across ERD, schemas, DDL, tests

findings.summary: trace_link/drift_event not repo-scoped: colliding logical_ids across repos corrupt links and staleness; both single-repo PBTs blind to it
findings.fix: ERD §3 repo columns, schemas, DDL, repo-scoped queries, cross-repo collision test

findings.summary: link-replacement semantics existed only as a code comment, not in the ERD
findings.fix: ERD §3 sentence: trace_link is replace-on-register

findings.summary: artifacts.ts re-declared enums inline instead of importing @kelson/schemas types
findings.fix: types imported from schemas package

findings.summary: PBT edit used constant content; spurious failure possible if generator produced it
findings.fix: suffix edit as in ART-1

findings.summary: survivable ingest failure left no sticky marker; later clean endSession would promote partial records into gate eligibility
findings.fix: safeIngest marks session degraded on failure + fail-then-clean-end test

findings.summary: merge_clean accepted live with zero correction-window machinery
findings.fix: merge_clean structurally refused in Phase 0 with test

findings.summary: code granted abandon edges (open/in_progress → abandoned) the PRD chain never defined
findings.fix: PRD §3 amended: abandoned reachable from any non-terminal state

findings.summary: correction_count semantics undefined in spec; increment wired to wrong edge
findings.fix: PRD §3 + ERD define it as ingestion-maintained from correction interventions; code wired accordingly

findings.summary: acceptance_signal writable on any transition via COALESCE
findings.fix: signal rejected unless transition is accepted

findings.summary: static network-pattern scan missed http2, bare specifiers, undici/axios, XMLHttpRequest/sendBeacon
findings.fix: patterns tightened; runtime recorder remains P0-7

findings.summary: PACK-1 left unmapped-path behavior undefined (README, stray dirs)
findings.fix: spec amended: unmapped paths other than pack.yaml/pack.sig refused fail-closed, naming the file

findings.summary: content-hash spec did not exclude pack.sig — signature would cover itself
findings.fix: spec §2 amended: pack.sig excluded; exclusion test in PACK-2.test.ts

findings.summary: listFiles emitted platform-sep paths: win32 hashes diverge and every nested path refused as unmapped
findings.fix: paths normalized to POSIX / before mapping/hashing; spec §1 pins POSIX paths

findings.summary: spec never defined which semver-range grammar counts as valid (hyphen ranges, x.x undefined)
findings.fix: grammar pinned in §1: comparator sets joined by ||; hyphen ranges/build metadata out in v1

findings.summary: F-007 discharged by diff but findings row still deferred and not staged with the fix
findings.fix: row flipped to fixed in the same commit

findings.summary: PACK-4 obligation said 'every field mutation' while §4 prose and impl exclude parent_hash
findings.fix: obligation amended: 'every content field mutation (parent_hash excluded)'

findings.summary: step boundary counted JSONL lines, not unique message ids — real transcripts emitted ~1.9 records/step and over-counted input tokens ~3x; manual exit check compared the parser to itself
findings.fix: TEL-1 amended to pin the boundary (unique message.id, last usage wins); parser dedupes; exit criterion re-run against independent dedup-by-id truth (327 steps, all four classes exact)

findings.summary: replacement obligation test was weaker than the removed kernel it.todo: arb had no message ids or duplicate lines, structurally unable to catch the over-count
findings.fix: arb generates duplicate-id line groups with decoy usage (last wins); real-shape regression fixture added

findings.summary: hook module-level imports sat outside try/catch — fresh clone before bun install would fail loud; no KERN-1 fault-injection test exists in any package
findings.fix: dynamic imports moved inside try in all three hooks; KERN-1 fault-injection matrix deferred to Phase 0.5 (this row records it)

findings.summary: session-end with a marker pointing at an unknown session id silently lost the transcript and leaked the marker file
findings.fix: falls back to beginSession when the marker id is unknown; marker removed unconditionally

findings.summary: parse PBT summed only in/out; combined four-class total was invariant under a cache_read/cache_write swap in the parser
findings.fix: per-class sum assertions in both the parse PBT and the ingestion test

findings.summary: neither the runtime recorder nor the static scan covered child processes or bun:ffi as network escape hatches
findings.fix: Bun.spawn, child_process, bun:ffi patterns added to both static scans

findings.summary: task board listed only TEL-1/TEL-2 for P0-7 though the diff also implements against ART-2, KERN-1, TEL-5
findings.fix: clauses added to the task row and commit message

findings.summary: CI PBT seed found StepEvent.unit_prices accepted arbitrary string keys; a __proto__ key round-trips through JSON but Zod v4 silently strips it — data dropped at a storage boundary, invisible to local runs (seed-dependent flake)
findings.fix: ERD pins unit_prices keys as lowercase identifier tokens; schema enforces the pattern (rejects ordinary bad keys) and the Zod proto-strip is pinned by test as structurally-unstorable; arb aligned to the valid domain

findings.summary: ART-3 admitted two materially different code-side drift baselines: frozen per-link hash vs last-synced index hash; the latter silently erases pending drift on index rebuild
findings.fix: ART-5 added pinning per-link frozen downstream_hash_at_link; ERD trace_link + §3 note updated

findings.summary: kelspec-dsl.md normative example violated its own rules: example check referenced undeclared ctx.count/ctx.windowRemainder (rejected by DSL-3), invariant used nonexistent sum() helper, §4 manifest entry count contradicted DSL-6
findings.fix: example declares count input + window_remainder observable; invariant check self-contained reduce; §4 corrected to 2 manifest entries

findings.summary: DSL-3 mutation obligation was seed-dependent: count===rate trigger vanishingly rare under random int pairs, so falsification passed or failed by luck between runs
findings.fix: pinned the triggering boundary as an explicit fc examples entry so falsification is deterministic

findings.summary: buildGate accepted override {by, reason} and discarded both — no record of who overrode or why existed anywhere, violating ART-4 recorded-override requirement; obligation test only checked resolution/resolved_at so it could not catch the drop
findings.fix: drift_event gains resolved_by/resolution_reason (ERD + migration 0002 + schema); buildGate persists both; ART-4 obligation and test extended to assert attribution round-trips

findings.summary: runVerify takes caller-supplied obligation thunks; manifest-driven discovery of touched clauses is not implemented and the narrowing was not in any deferral note
findings.fix: narrowing recorded in P1-8 board note; manifest-driven obligation discovery deferred until pipeline stages exist (Phase 3 context compiler gives touched-file sets)

findings.summary: ART-4 text named only confirmed as blocking while buildGate blocked all non-inferred authorities; authored behavior was pinned only by an implementation comment
findings.fix: ART-4 respecified as stale non-inferred (authored or confirmed); obligation matrix and test gained the authored row

findings.summary: claudeExecutor forwarded operator HOME/USER into the session under test while SEC-1 stated an unqualified temp-HOME guarantee for worktree; the SEC-1 test only checked the generic exec path (F-031 self-referential pattern)
findings.fix: SEC-1 respecified with the explicit claude-auth exception (exact 4-var set); passthrough extracted to claudeSessionEnv() and pinned by a new SEC-1 test case

findings.summary: migration 0003 added eval_run.executor and eval_task_result.repeat_index but ERD §6 (owner of the data model) was not rippled
findings.fix: ERD §6 EVAL_RUN gains executor, EVAL_TASK_RESULT gains repeat_index with clause annotations

findings.summary: the audited-violation-event halves of SEC-1/SEC-2 obligations are unimplemented; escape/egress tests assert blocking only
findings.fix: deferral now recorded in both obligations (audit events land with Phase 4 telemetry expansion; Phase 2 records failures in eval_task_result.check_results)

findings.summary: EVP §2.1 said claude cost comes from transcript token usage priced at run time; code uses the CLI self-reported total_cost_usd, and a zero-exit unparseable session silently recorded cost 0
findings.fix: spec row now states the self-reported-cost source; unparseable zero-exit output is a session failure

findings.summary: KELSON_COST_FILE pointed at a host path that does not exist inside container-profile mounts, silently breaking command-executor cost reporting in containers
findings.fix: env var maps to /workspace/.kelson-cost under container isolation; runner reads the host side

findings.summary: kelson.lock ponytail entry hash is synthetic (sha256 of the string ponytail@4.7.0) — no pack content dir exists yet for CC plugins, so PACK-1 content hashing cannot apply
findings.fix: provenance recorded here: placeholder hash until plugin packs get content dirs (Phase 5 packaging); lockfile exists to pin the P2-10 exit-run config

findings.summary: EVP-6 obligation cases lived inside EVT-3.test.ts violating the one-clause-one-file convention; EVAL-3 contained a vacuously-true placeholder assertion
findings.fix: EVP-6.test.ts split out; EVAL-3 asserts the fresh quarantine event for the flaky task

findings.summary: kelson eval publish subcommand was absent from the UX command surface
findings.fix: UX §3 eval command list gains publish

findings.summary: base-url override dummied only the API key; the OAuth token still reached arbitrary override endpoints, outside SEC-1 auth-exception justification (operator account only)
findings.fix: EVP-8 respecified to withhold operator credentials under override endpoints; buildClaudeEnv drops OAuth + real key when ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL set; env-precedence obligation case added

findings.summary: --base-url without --model was silently discarded, running real-spend sessions the operator believed were local
findings.fix: CLI rejects --base-url without --model with a usage diagnostic

findings.summary: the dummy-key-wins merge order existed only as a code comment; no test covered claude-executor env precedence
findings.fix: env construction extracted to buildClaudeEnv and pinned by an obligation case seeding real credentials in the parent env

findings.summary: CTX-1 admitted two empty-bundle readings (synthetic preamble recording nonzero vs empty string recording zero) and left the tokenizer identity unpinned across implementations
findings.fix: CTX-5 added: one pinned tokenizer both sides recorded on events, session usage never participates, empty bundle records exactly zero

findings.summary: CTX-4 admitted diverging continue-headroom semantics (one-more-budget vs next whole multiple) and unnamed headless cap-exhausted terminal state
findings.fix: RPOL-6 + §3.1 added pinning latched thresholds, durable pause, one-budget continue headroom, headless escalate-then-block, minimal attribution set

findings.summary: per-section-sum accounting cannot satisfy the 2% bound on small bundles — a single BPE seam merge exceeds the whole tolerance (fast-check shrunk a 5-item counterexample); CTX-5 as first written pinned the failing derivation as the recorded count
findings.fix: recorded count respecified as exact whole-text tokenization; per-section manifest sums become the independent verification route; CTX-1 PBT verifies both routes

findings.summary: budget event queries ordered by ULID, which does not sort within one millisecond — burst-ordering assertions (1x-then-2x-then-pause) failed nondeterministically
findings.fix: event-stream queries order by rowid (insertion order); comment pins the constraint

findings.summary: ERD not rippled: ROUTING_DECISION diverged from shipped table, bundle/budget event tables had no ERD entities, and the capability-override vs rule-target distinction was unrecorded
findings.fix: ERD §5 rewritten to the shipped model; routing_decision gains matched_by (rule|capability) in schema+migration+code

findings.summary: eval routing integration fabricated langCounts {typescript:1} — a dominance never derived from files — so recorded RTR-1 vectors carried a made-up feature; per-task extraction is a real deferral
findings.fix: fabricated count dropped (lang honestly falls back to unknown); per-task feature extraction deferred to Phase 4 pipeline, recorded here

findings.summary: post-hoc headless resolution recorded triage_resolved action=escalate that would never be acted on — false telemetry; unparseable usage silently skipped accounting
findings.fix: post-session 2x pauses now stay open (the durable pause is the honest record); unparseable usage logs a diagnostic

findings.summary: CTX-1 2% bound was discharged self-referentially (recorded and actual were the same countTokens call on the same string) and the designated per-section verification route was never summed
findings.fix: realistic-size fixture asserts manifest sums + separators within 2% of the recorded count — the independent derivation CTX-5 designates

findings.summary: §3.1 pinned >= for all thresholds but both divergence readers and CTX-4 text use strict > for 1x; code was strict, spec said otherwise, equality boundary untested
findings.fix: §3.1 respecified (1x strictly exceeds, 2x inclusive); boundary cases added (==budget no event, ==2x pauses)

findings.summary: targetBudget derivation existed only as a code comment and could grab a rule from a different step; escalate() triage outcome is a sentinel with no durable pause event
findings.fix: derivation pinned in RPOL §3 (vector-matching rule first); durable pause wiring for router-triggered triage deferred to the Phase 4 pipeline that owns step state

findings.summary: readInitialLadder ordered by ISO timestamp — the F-060 within-millisecond tie hazard reintroduced
findings.fix: ORDER BY rowid

findings.summary: routing-default pack had no pack.yaml (PACK-1 loader would refuse it) and the policy loads by raw path without verifyPackContent against the lock hash
findings.fix: pack.yaml added (loader verified), lock hash refreshed to real content hash; verifyPackContent wiring on the routing load path deferred to Phase 5 supply chain

findings.summary: tier fallback to T0 (exploration-permissive) was undeclared in the RPOL-2 feature table — fail-open under the RTR-3 guard
findings.fix: table row pinned: touching no clause-governed logic yields T0 (no criticality floor exists to inherit)

findings.summary: blocked existed only as in-process monitor state — another process could not distinguish blocked from running
findings.fix: stepTriageState derives running|paused|blocked from the event stream; obligation case added

findings.summary: --model with --routing-pack silently clobbered the session-model override while the manifest still recorded it
findings.fix: runEval refuses the combination pre-flight with a diagnostic

findings.summary: recordBundleMiss counted a synthetic frame never delivered to context
findings.fix: miss tokens count exactly the delivered content

findings.summary: LOOP-1 admitted diverging link schemes, check moments (creation vs pre-gate only), and rejection shapes (document mutation vs evented state transition)
findings.fix: LOOP-8 pins the two link grammars, both check moments, stated-location-only resolution, atomic evented rejection

findings.summary: LOOP-3 admitted two different bootstrap constructions (percentile-of-difference vs pooled-null — materially different p-values) and two attribution algorithms; both readers independently invented the same min-8 floor and conjunctive window closure, which were unpinned
findings.fix: LOOP-9 + §9.2.1 pin pooled-null seeded bootstrap, min-8, conjunctive closure, inter-apply-stratum attribution, single-revert-per-window, content-hash quarantine

findings.summary: CI changelog check fell back to HEAD comparison exactly when tampering was detected — a contiguous history rewrite passed CI; comparison also used origin/main instead of the merge base
findings.fix: merge-base comparison, no fallback; script hard-fails on unresolvable base ref, skips only when the changelog does not exist at the base

findings.summary: direction-aware gate was unsound: hurts means inferior on EITHER metric, so a fpar-improving cost-inferior pack auto-approved its own disable despite the disable being fpar-inferior
findings.fix: EVP §5 pins candidate-as-side-A; evaluateGate recomputes the decision table from per-task results with sides swapped when needed — verdict labels never reinterpreted

findings.summary: I2 cap off-by-one (3rd monitor refused vs TLA Monitor enabled at <K) and non-transactional openMonitor orphaned an open record with the proposal stuck in applied
findings.fix: cap counts exclude the proposal own record, checked before insert, insert+transition atomic; cap-boundary obligation case added

findings.summary: the stratum-starved/re-measure simulation required by LOOP-9 obligation did not exist
findings.fix: starved-stratum case added: revert last-applied only, survivor re-measures on fresh sessions

findings.summary: attribution silently narrowed: slice(-2) dropped older simultaneous suspects and isolation read FPAR only, collapsing TPAC regressions to revert-last-applied
findings.fix: §9.2.1 pins iterative two-most-recent-per-sweep semantics; isolation is metric-aware (triggering family)

findings.summary: suite min_sample: 6 contradicted EVP §5 rule 4 hard n>=20 and was recorded only as a YAML comment — the Goodhart pattern the PRD risk table names
findings.fix: EVP §5 defines suite-configurable minimum with a hard schema floor of 6 and requires a recorded decision row (this row); EVAL-2 test covers the config path

findings.summary: the gate was advisory: approve/apply never consulted the gate basis, resolving the LOOP-2/EVAL-5 tension in code without spec text
findings.fix: LOOP-2 respecified with the recorded human-override path; transition-to-approved structurally refuses loop proposals lacking a passing basis or human override; refusal case tested

findings.summary: session selection + manifest recording of replayed session ids unimplemented; test title overclaimed
findings.fix: gap recorded here — lands with the replay driver once sessions accumulate snapshots; aggregate.session_ids is the current record

findings.summary: snapshot environment manifest missing lockfile hash, kelson version, model ids per EVP §4
findings.fix: lockfile_hash + kelson_version added at capture; model ids remain a recorded gap until session-end enrichment

findings.summary: UX §3 loop command surface and PRD §9.2 state line + phase table not rippled for the new subcommands and quarantined->proposed release edge
findings.fix: UX command list, §9.2 release edge, and Phase 4 clause range updated

findings.summary: the corrected candidate-side gate had no caller passing candidateSide — the CLI evaluated every proposal as side A, resurfacing the F-076 unsoundness one layer up
findings.fix: candidateSide is now a required parameter (no default); CLI derives B for standard ablate geometry with --candidate-side override; side-swap obligation case added; stale direction comment deleted; loop approve requires an explicit --reason

findings.summary: SPEC-4 admitted opposite float-comparison rules (harness epsilon vs bit-exact) and two invented interface contracts (kelspec declares no functions)
findings.fix: DSL-7 + kelspec §5 pin per-clause harness exports, bit-exact comparison (spec licenses imprecision only via domains/nondeterministic), spec-hash seeding, obligation gate

findings.summary: SEC-4 surface-addressing was unspecified — both readers convergently invented the same path-prefix table, which was recorded nowhere
findings.fix: pack-format §3.1 pins the path-prefix table, ceiling semantics, atomic refusal

findings.summary: promotionCandidate 50-consecutive streak was fake — recent.every() re-evaluated the same point-in-time weights, empirically returning true after 49 straight failures
findings.fix: real chronological EMA replay over both arms; margin must hold after each of the last 50 outcomes; late-crosser obligation fixture added

findings.summary: docs allowlist regex unanchored — README.mdx, LICENSE-evil.sh, CHANGELOG.mdfoo/ bypassed fail-closed refusal
findings.fix: anchored exact-match set plus docs/ prefix; near-miss fixtures added

findings.summary: survival clock reset on identical-content re-registration (updated_at bumped unconditionally) and session count ignored repo
findings.fix: updated_at conditional on content-hash change; repo-filtered session count; identical-reingest fixture

findings.summary: metadata-only->patch row unimplementable (manifest inside content hash) and kelson pack lint + CI enforcement did not exist
findings.fix: entries_hash split from content_hash; patch fixture; kelson pack lint CLI; scripts/pack-bump-check.mjs in CI vs merge base

findings.summary: PACK-2 obligation test stale ((partial) + it.todo) while Ed25519 landed elsewhere; untrusted flag unimplemented
findings.fix: PACK-2.test.ts owns the signature suite; loadPack signature option returns untrusted; telemetry-event propagation of the flag recorded here as a deferral (needs install/session wiring)

findings.summary: README/package.json declared MIT while LICENSE is Apache-2.0 — a license switch smuggled into an implementation diff
findings.fix: reverted to Apache-2.0 (registry-before-versions rule: read the LICENSE file, not memory)

findings.summary: scanner missed the canonical Ignore-all-previous-instructions string, leaving exactly 19/20 = 0.95 — threshold margin fully consumed at baseline
findings.fix: regex accepts stacked qualifiers ((all|previous|prior)\s+)*; 20/20 with margin restored

findings.summary: DSL-7 fixture rows discharged via gate-stripped {...spec, clauses: []} casts — unreachable through the production API
findings.fix: probeImplementations exposed as the sanctioned gate-free stage (spec amended); test uses it, casts gone

findings.summary: OSS-1 obligation names macOS + Linux; CI was ubuntu-only; UX J0 confirm-panel contract not honored by the CLI form
findings.fix: macos-latest install job added; UX J0 amended: plan-panel confirm is TUI-only, CLI form is non-interactive + idempotent + non-destructive

findings.summary: operator docs referenced nonexistent commands (kelson drift promote, kelson pack lint)
findings.fix: pack lint now exists; drift promote marked as shipping with the TUI

findings.summary: flaky window read historical results without schema-version check; shared schemas lacked paired round-trip arbitraries
findings.fix: flakiness window refuses mixed versions; SharedStepEvent/SharedSessionEvent arbitraries added

findings.summary: identical-reingest fixture was vacuous — 2099 fixture sessions vs wall-clock updated_at meant even the regressed unconditional bump passed (verification-independence rule, F-031 class)
findings.fix: direct column assertion: updated_at byte-identical across identical re-ingest, moves on content change

findings.summary: UX-4 NO_COLOR/plain-degrade assertions were unfailable: only never-coloring renderers were asserted, NO_COLOR never set, sink strip untested (F-100 class)
findings.fix: discriminating fixtures: paint() across the TTY/NO_COLOR boundary via isTTY stub, sink strip asserted on captured stdout

findings.summary: ADR-0003 rows stale after §7 amendment: OpenTUI described as the whole component layer, commander listed as adopted but never shipped
findings.fix: ADR-0003 amended: OpenTUI interactive-only; commander struck, hand-rolled parseArgs + COMMANDS table recorded (UX-8 identity)

findings.summary: skipLibCheck added repo-wide without a recorded decision
findings.fix: recorded in ADR-0003: third-party d.ts skipped, own sources fully strict

findings.summary: traceView empty_verb named kelson index rebuild, which is in UX §3 but not dispatchable — copied verb would error
findings.fix: verb points at kelson init until index rebuild ships

findings.summary: static-asset traversal guard checked .. on the already-joined path — dead code; protection rested on URL normalization alone
findings.fix: guard asserts resolved path startsWith(staticDir)

findings.summary: repo-first store resolution shipped as §8 prose with no clause ID or obligation — behavior governed by nothing, dodged spec-lint
findings.fix: UX-13 minted as EARS bullet with obligation; §8 references it

findings.summary: resolution test was free-floating (test/ui-db-resolution.test.ts), untraceable to an obligation
findings.fix: moved to test/obligations/UX-13.test.ts

findings.summary: resolver test did not discriminate the createUiServer wiring — reverting the default to the user store left tests green
findings.fix: wiring fixture: seeded temp repo store served through /api/loop with no dbPath

findings.summary: unexplained 100644->100755 mode flip on cli/src/index.ts bundled into the diff (bun link side effect)
findings.fix: intent stated in commit message; bin entry executable is correct

findings.summary: launcher SelectRenderable shipped with no flexGrow/height — laid out to height 1 in the flex column, clipping every command row (user saw a blank menu bar). The interactive TTY render was never exercised; only the pure model and non-TTY path were tested.
findings.fix: flexGrow:1 on the menu; buildLauncher extracted and driven by a headless test-renderer asserting menu.height >= option count (measured 1 buggy vs 23 fixed)

findings.summary: Divergence testing: AGT-2 split on resume-after-done (no-op vs error) and empty pause reason (store vs reject) -> AGT-5/AGT-6 added; PERM-1 converged on 13 probes but both readers flagged blanket-deny defeated by specific allow -> deny-trump amendment
findings.fix: AGT-5, AGT-6 clarifying clauses; PERM-1 deny-trump + explicit specificity metric

findings.summary: Ollama smoke run recorded all-zero token counts: openai-compatible adapter omits stream_options.include_usage by default, so usage never arrived and zeros were ingested as real values
findings.fix: includeUsage: true in resolve.ts openai-compatible provider config; verified live (544/89 tokens on re-run)

findings.summary: lifecycle() derived paused from unresolved tool calls while AGT-6 pinned it to permission_request-without-decision; the two disagreed on answered-but-unexecuted asks and step-limit suspensions were unresumable (resume threw on a state the runtime itself reported paused)
findings.fix: AGT-6 amended to chain-suspension derivation (last assistant message has tool calls => paused); lifecycle() aligned; discriminating 5-state obligation test

findings.summary: kelson chat --continue was unimplemented and the SES-4 obligation exercised appendEvent directly instead of the operator surface (F-085 pattern)
findings.fix: --continue implemented on run and chat via continueSession(); SES-4 obligation relocated to cli as a run --continue e2e; clause text names both surfaces

findings.summary: setupAgent guarded missing config but not missing credential: an anthropic model with no key died inside the SDK mid-request without naming kelson auth login
findings.fix: fail-fast credential check for the anthropic provider in setupAgent + PROV-4 obligation branch

findings.summary: chat shell never dispatched tool_result/step_cost reducer messages (tool events and cost footer dead in live chat); AGT-3 telemetry hardcodes sdlc_step=build, effort=medium, budget_tokens=1M until Phase 9 wires routing/budgets
findings.fix: onToolResult/onStepCost callbacks added to StepDeps and wired in chat; budget-field constants recorded here as the Phase 9 replacement list

findings.summary: UX-17 divergence: no material split (both readers converged on all 6 probes) but both committed to semantics the clause left unstated (switch-while-busy timing, call-issue attribution, no-op same-model select, chain-derived model on resume); sibling cross-check exposed that --continue would have re-read the config default instead of the chain-derived model
findings.fix: UX-17 pinned with the readers verbatim attribution rule; SES-4 continue path will derive model from the chain

findings.summary: Widening the Executor Zod enum left migration 0003 eval_run CHECK (executor IN claude,command) behind — the enum typechecks but the INSERT dies at runtime; caught by the EVP-9 e2e obligation test, fixed by rebuild migration 0009
findings.fix: migration 0009 rebuilds eval_run with the widened CHECK; convention added to CLAUDE.md (enum widening greps migrations for matching CHECKs)

findings.summary: apiExecutor set credential=null for override endpoints, but the AI SDK anthropic adapter falls back to process.env.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY — leaking the operator real key as x-api-key to an arbitrary endpoint (probe-confirmed). buildClaudeEnv substitutes a dummy; the native path did not
findings.fix: substitute {type:api_key,key:kelson-local} when overridden; EVP-9 obligation extended with a capture-server assertion on the header value (verification-independence)

findings.summary: UX-17 divergence-pinned switch-while-busy was unimplemented — the reducer silently dropped all busy-time submits, and applying a switch mid-step would orphan the event off the reconstructed chain
findings.fix: re-pinned UX-17 to paused/idle (the serialized-turn TUI cannot latch mid-stream); busy submits now rejected with a message; appendModelSwitch guards a moved head; busy-rejection obligation added

findings.summary: apiExecutor recorded auth_kind but never passed authKind into runTurn deps, so the PROV-7 401 re-mint diagnostic could never fire on subscription-auth eval runs
findings.fix: wired authKind: authKindOf(credential) into the executor deps

findings.summary: AGT-7 divergence: no material split across 8 probes (both readers converged) but both independently assumed unstated load-bearing semantics — done-gate over the session-accumulated touched set (not just this step), evaluated every step; cache key = combined hash of all a clauses governed files; fail clears by content-addressed lookup not eviction; missing obligation = fail; run-before-done-check ordering
findings.fix: folded the converged commitments into AGT-7 verbatim; implementability cross-checked (governed file only changes via a touching tool call, so the gate lookup is always a hit — re-hash, not re-run)

findings.summary: done-gate bypass: modifiedPaths tracked only write/edit, so a bash call breaking a governed file escaped the obligation runner and the done-gate missed (cachedStatus null at the new hash != failing) — wrongly allowing done
findings.fix: a bash call in the batch forces a re-check of all governed clauses (content-addressed cache re-runs only changed ones); AGT-7 clause updated; F-123 bash-bypass obligation added; classifier both-failed nuance fixed to match AGT-9

findings.summary: macOS cwd/realpath trace-link mismatch: AGT-9-cli seeded trace links under /tmp but the spawned CLI process.cwd() resolved to /private/tmp, so loadSpecContext found no links and the SpecContext was empty — recurring realpath class (also hit tool containment in Phase 6)
findings.fix: realpathSync the seed path; CLAUDE.md gotcha added — realpath any path used as a DB key or containment prefix from a symlinked temp dir or subprocess cwd

findings.summary: AGT-11 budget-triage divergence: no material split (both readers converged) but both independently pinned unstated load-bearing semantics — monitor seeded ONCE per session at step 1 (cumulative, not per-step; probe 7 the decider), record-before-tools, continue resumes deferred tools without a second model call, escalate re-runs the same step, re_spec interactive-only
findings.fix: folded converged semantics into AGT-11 verbatim

findings.summary: recordStepOutcome fired for every routed step, not just mechanical/T0 (AGT-10 scope)
findings.fix: gated recordStepOutcome to mechanical/T0 matching the exploration scope

findings.summary: headless budget triage inert: kernel BudgetMonitor.resolve clears the pause + grants headroom ONLY for continue, not escalate/block — so resolveHeadless(escalate) left the monitor paused, the !budget.paused guard then permanently disabled record(), accounting died and the run went unbounded to step_limit; resolveHeadless also never returned block (escalationDepth fixed at 0)
findings.fix: headless now grants up to BUDGET_HEADLESS_CAP kernel continue extensions (the only resolve that clears+grants) then blocks with reason budget:blocked; AGT-11 clause corrected; discriminating block-at-cap test added

findings.summary: SES-7 BranchSummary omitted lifecycle (clause outcome = lifecycle + last text); SES-8 from_event pointed at the synthetic summary id on a re-compact (non-existent in store)
findings.fix: added lifecycle to BranchSummary + CLI + test; SES-8 from_event resolves to the first real covered event via realIds set

findings.summary: SES-4 obligation promises the empty-final-turn --continue case through kelson run (operator surface), but staged coverage was a unit test on toMessages only — the CLI path never exercised the empty-turn resume
findings.fix: cli SES-4 test extended: empty first turn via mock, --continue exits 0, wire-level assertion (captured request bodies) that no empty-content assistant message reaches the provider

findings.summary: contained() tail re-attach used existing.slice(parent.length+1), off by one at the root: /x<realCwd>/f re-attached to <realCwd>/f and was wrongly ACCEPTED as an aliased in-workspace write at a path the model never named
findings.fix: basename(existing) for the tail component; discriminating fixture: write to /x<realCwd>/probe.txt must throw escapes-the-workspace and create nothing

findings.summary: AGT-4 obligation names symlink refusal for read/write/edit/ls but the test discriminated only write
findings.fix: parametrized symlink-refusal test over all four tools asserting the escape error per tool

findings.summary: InitResult/PackLintResult shipped without paired arbitraries in roundtrip.test.ts, breaking the schema-arbitrary pairing convention the analogous RunResult follows
findings.fix: both arbitraries added to the roundtrip suite

findings.summary: board rows REV-8/REV-9 carried clauses:[""] (empty-string entry) and board.mjs task re-marking clobbered completed_at, falsifying history on repair
findings.fix: board.mjs: --clauses "" now yields []; completed_at preserved on re-mark; both rows repaired through the tool

findings.summary: EVP-11 manifest sentence (per-agent model ids + auth_kind marks) was undischargeable as written: modelVersions defaulted to {} with no caller populating it and SessionOutcome has no reporting channel
findings.fix: clause amended to session_model-when-set (PROV-3 never-guessed) with auth_kind provenance delegated to PROV-6 session telemetry; runBench gained the model plumb (ANTHROPIC_MODEL to both agents, manifest records session_model); obligation + tests extended

findings.summary: dimension upsert ran before the all-quarantined refusal, so a refused run still wrote eval_suite/benchmark_task rows while the clause claimed refusal writes nothing
findings.fix: quarantine read + empty-set refusal moved above the upsert — the clause is now literally true

findings.summary: renderVerdict computed the underpowered deficit against the hard-coded default minSample=20 while runs gate on suite.min_sample/gateOpts — a min_sample=6 suite at n=4 rendered a 16-task deficit instead of 2 (pre-existing in the eval path, newly wired into bench)
findings.fix: effective minSample threaded through BenchRunResult and EvalRunResult into renderVerdict on both surfaces

findings.summary: sessions received KELSON_BENCH_REPEAT and KELSON_ENABLED_PACKS, neither named in EVP-11, and the obligation fixture keys on the former (de facto contract)
findings.fix: clause amended to name all four env vars and their cross-side identity semantics

findings.summary: ERD §2 trigger-enforced append-only enumeration not extended with bench_task_result while §6 pointed readers at §2 for that property
findings.fix: bench_task_result added to the ERD §2 list

findings.summary: the non-zero-on-refusal commitment rode an uncaught throw (raw stack, untested) out of main()
findings.fix: benchCommand catches pre-flight refusals into fail() (kelson: diagnostic) and UX-18.test.ts asserts the refusal exit code and stack-free stderr

findings.summary: in-shell | head -n 200 swallowed the search exit code (pipeline status = head's), making the error branch dead in both branches — an invalid regex returned (no matches) instead of exit 2 + stderr, contradicting the clause's fallback-preserved-verbatim claim
findings.fix: cap moved entirely into the tool (capNotice line+char caps); clause names the pipe-masking hazard; bad-regex fixture asserts exit 2 under both branches

findings.summary: exactly-200-line untruncated results claimed the cap notice (post-shell, 200-exact and clipped were indistinguishable)
findings.fix: JS-side cap sees the full result; notice only on actual truncation; exactly-200 fixture added

findings.summary: all=true splice-ordering fixture was non-discriminating (same-length old/new — front-to-back splicing would still pass, F-100 class)
findings.fix: length-changing multi-window fixture added (3-line new over 2-line old, two windows)

findings.summary: the streamed gate armed only on text-delta/tool-call while the clause said first streamed part — reasoning/tool-input parts would still retry
findings.fix: clause re-pinned to first streamed content part (text delta or tool call — the parts the loop consumes)

findings.summary: detached HEAD misparse: git branch --show-current prints nothing, so the dirty count masqueraded as the branch name in the env block
findings.fix: two structural execs; git line emitted only on a non-empty branch name

findings.summary: no-rg PATH fixture hardcoded /usr/bin:/bin — on a runner with rg in /usr/bin the fallback fixtures exercise the wrong branch
findings.fix: no-rg PATH built by excluding the resolved rg directory; obligation names the rule

findings.summary: the non-empty-context conditional had no discriminating fixture (empty-messages guard untested)
findings.fix: root-only-chain case added: instructions marked, messages empty, no error; obligation extended

findings.summary: caps stated as bytes but measured in UTF-16 code units (.length/.slice) — multibyte content overshoots up to 3x
findings.fix: caps restated as characters in both clauses and notices

findings.summary: CRLF files: tolerant replacements land LF while untouched lines keep CRLF — mixed endings, spec silent
findings.fix: pinned out-of-scope in the clause (tolerant replacements land LF in v1)

findings.summary: SMOKE-TEST ROW (will verify flag form then keep as postmortem record): findings flag form added to board.mjs
findings.fix: flag form self-test

findings.summary: Dropping packages/ui typescript ^6.0.3 devDep converges ui-build on root TS 5.9.3 — a compiler-major downgrade on fresh install that local gates did not exercise (stale 6.0.3 symlink survived bun install)
findings.fix: Intentional: dual conflicting TS majors was the over-engineering finding; auditor force-compiled ui with root 5.9.3 clean; CI fresh-install path confirms post-push

findings.summary: scanPack readdirSync rewrite changed symlink semantics: old walk read file symlinks (threw on dangling), new isFile() filter skips symlinks entirely; multi-dir traversal order may differ
findings.fix: Accepted: scanner scope now exactly equals loadPack isFile() scope (packs.ts:57) so nothing loadable escapes the scan; SEC-5 obligation is order-independent; symlink fixture optional follow-up

findings.summary: EVP-12 delay-reversal fixture was vacuous: Workspace.exec was spawnSync, so cells serialized on the event loop and completion order always equaled submission order; the concurrency clause delivered zero overlap for built-in executors
findings.fix: Workspace gained execAsync (Bun.spawn); command/claude session executors run async; obligation test now records actual completion order via an injected async executor and asserts it differs from persisted order

findings.summary: Image-pull single-flight sentence had no implementation and no obligation coverage; unviolatable only while execution was accidentally serial
findings.fix: Clause amended: container profile clamps effective concurrency to 1 via exported effectiveConcurrency (runEval consults it); single-flight acquisition deferred until container concurrency lands

findings.summary: Override-endpoint definition (any base_url on an anthropic entry) lived only in a code comment; clause said credential-own-provider without defining detection
findings.fix: Pinned into PROV-10 clause text: any base_url reads as override, official endpoint spelled by omitting base_url

findings.summary: SEC-7 obligation overclaimed: raw literal /../ is clamped by the runtime pre-handler and serves the SPA fallback, never 404; only encoded-slash forms reach containment
findings.fix: Obligation reworded to name encoded-slash forms as the 404 cases and content-absence for raw literals

findings.summary: askRuleOf (payload-to-render bridge) was module-local in the OpenTUI shell and untested; payload shape drift would silently render default for every matched rule
findings.fix: Moved to chat/model.ts (pure) and tested against the exact payload shape loop.ts writes

findings.summary: PRD §14 intro still enumerated two attack surfaces after §14.3 added a third
findings.fix: Intro updated to three surfaces including local surfaces

findings.summary: --concurrency abc parsed to NaN, yielding zero workers and an internal cell-produced-no-outcome throw instead of a usage error
findings.fix: CLI validates positive-integer concurrency and dies with usage message

findings.summary: Pre-existing PROV-5 test second case pointed a real token at a base_url capture server — it asserted the F-119 leak class as expected behavior
findings.fix: Reworked to capture via injected fetch on a no-base_url entry, matching PROV-5 obligation wording; PROV-10 test covers the withheld case

findings.summary: workspace.execAsync used Bun.spawn timeout; on Linux CI killing a subprocess whose piped stdout is being read leaves the read pending forever (oven-sh/bun#1498), so the EVP-1 timeout task never returned — green on macOS, hung in CI
findings.fix: execAsync now uses node:child_process execFile with timeout/killSignal/maxBuffer; timedOut derives from err.signal

findings.summary: the exactly-200-line cap-boundary test ran through withRg, but CI runners have no ripgrep, so it fell to the grep fallback which also matches the .gitignore'd twin — 201 lines, cap notice fired, test red since it landed
findings.fix: boundary test now runs on the fallback branch (present on every runner) with the fixture sized to 200 total matches; mutation-checked against a >= off-by-one
